text: Reaction of alkyl halides with Mg in dry ether gives:
explanation: Grignard reagent: R–X + Mg → R–MgX (dry ether). RMgX is a powerful nucleophile/base used in organic synthesis.
name: Grignard Reagent
subject: che

text: If \( f(x) = x^3 \), then \( f \) is:
explanation: \( f(-x) = (-x)^3 = -x^3 = -f(x) \). So \(f\) is an odd function.
name: Odd Function Check
subject: maths

text: In the AC generator, the EMF produced is:
explanation: AC generator: \( \varepsilon = NBA\omega\sin(\omega t) = \varepsilon_0\sin(\omega t) \), where \(\varepsilon_0 = NBA\omega\) is peak EMF.
name: AC Generator EMF
subject: phy

text: Which of the following is NOT a colligative property?
explanation: Optical rotation depends on the nature of solute (chiral molecules), not on the number of particles.
name: Non-Colligative Property
subject: che

text: Kepler's third law states: \( T^2 \propto \)
explanation: Kepler's 3rd Law: \( T^2 \propto r^3 \) (semi-major axis). \( \frac{T_1^2}{T_2^2} = \frac{r_1^3}{r_2^3} \).
name: Kepler's Third Law
subject: phy

text: KMnO₄ in acidic medium acts as:
explanation: In acidic medium: \( \text{MnO}_4^- + 8\text{H}^+ + 5e^- \to \text{Mn}^{2+} + 4\text{H}_2\text{O} \). KMnO₄ is a powerful oxidant. Mn: +7 → +2.
name: KMnO₄ in Acidic Medium
subject: che

text: The impulse-momentum theorem states:
explanation: Impulse \( J = F\Delta t = \Delta p = mv - mu \). Explains why airbags (increase \(\Delta t\)) reduce force in collisions.
name: Impulse-Momentum Theorem
subject: phy

text: The rate of reaction is defined as:
options: Increase in concentration of products per unit time
explanation: Rate \( = -\frac{d[A]}{dt} = +\frac{d[P]}{dt} \). Negative sign for reactants (decreasing), positive for products.
name: Rate of Reaction
subject: che

text: If \( \alpha, \beta, \gamma \) are roots of \( x^3 - 6x^2 + 11x - 6 = 0 \), then \( \alpha + \beta + \gamma = \)
explanation: For cubic \( x^3 + px^2 + qx + r = 0 \), sum of roots \( = -p = -(-6) = 6 \)
name: Sum of Roots of Cubic
subject: maths

text: The number of atoms per unit cell in a simple cubic (SC) structure is:
explanation: SC: 8 corner atoms × (1/8) = 1 atom per unit cell.
name: Atoms in SC Unit Cell
subject: che

text: The time period of a simple pendulum is:
explanation: Simple pendulum: \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{l/g} \). Independent of mass and amplitude (for small oscillations).
name: Simple Pendulum Period
subject: phy

text: The floor function \( \lfloor 3.7 \rfloor = \)
explanation: \( \lfloor x \rfloor \) = greatest integer \( \leq x \). So \( \lfloor 3.7 \rfloor = 3 \)
name: Floor Function
subject: maths

text: The equation of the circle passing through origin with center \((3,4)\) is:
explanation: \( r = \sqrt{9+16}=5 \). Circle: \((x-3)^2+(y-4)^2=25\) expands to \(x^2+y^2-6x-8y=0\). Both A and D are correct, but A is the general form.
name: Circle Through Origin
subject: maths

text: In a p-n junction, the depletion region is formed by:
options: Diffusion of majority carriers across junction, leaving ionised atoms
explanation: Depletion region: electrons from n-side diffuse to p-side, holes from p-side to n-side. Ionised donors (n-side) and acceptors (p-side) remain → built-in potential barrier.
name: Depletion Region Formation
subject: phy

explanation: Dividing \( \cosh^2x - \sinh^2x = 1 \) by \( \cosh^2x \): \( 1 - \tanh^2x = \text{sech}^2x \)
name: sech² Identity
subject: maths

text: For an ideal gas, the adiabatic process follows:
explanation: Adiabatic process: \( PV^\gamma = \text{const} \), where \( \gamma = C_p/C_v > 1 \). More steep than isothermal curve on P-V diagram.
name: Adiabatic Process Equation
subject: phy

text: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\,dx = \)
explanation: Standard result: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}\,dx = \sin^{-1}x+C \)
name: Arcsin Integral
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = \)
explanation: Power Rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}(x^n) = nx^{n-1} \)
name: Power Rule
subject: maths

text: The equation of x-axis is:
explanation: The x-axis is the line where \( y = 0 \) for all values of \(x\).
name: Equation of Axes
subject: maths

explanation: \( \sin^{-1} \) is an odd function: \( \sin^{-1}(-x) = -\sin^{-1}(x) \).
name: Odd Function Property
subject: maths

text: The internal resistance \(r\) of a battery can be found using:
explanation: Terminal voltage: \( V = E - Ir \). When external \(R\) is connected: \( I = E/(R+r) \) and \( V = IR = E-Ir \).
name: Internal Resistance
subject: phy

text: The time of flight of a projectile launched with speed \(u\) at angle \(\theta\) is:
explanation: Time of flight: \( T = \frac{2u\sin\theta}{g} \). At maximum height, vertical velocity = 0, then returns.
name: Time of Flight
subject: phy

text: The hybridisation of carbon in \( \text{BF}_3 \) (Boron, not Carbon) is:
explanation: Boron in \(\text{BF}_3\): 3 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs → \(sp^2\) hybridisation → trigonal planar.
name: Hybridisation in BF₃
subject: che

text: If a constant \(c\) is added to each observation, the variance:
explanation: Adding a constant shifts all values equally; deviations from mean remain unchanged. Variance is unaffected.
name: Variance under Addition of Constant
subject: maths

text: Which is the most abundant element in the universe?
explanation: Hydrogen is the most abundant element in the universe (~75% by mass). It is the fuel for stars via nuclear fusion.
name: Most Abundant in Universe
subject: che

text: The sum of slopes of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) is:
explanation: If \(m_1,m_2\) are slopes: \( m_1+m_2 = -\frac{2h}{b} \) and \( m_1m_2 = \frac{a}{b} \) (Vieta's).
name: Sum of Slopes
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \tan\theta = \tan\alpha \) is:
explanation: \( \tan\theta = \tan\alpha \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + \alpha,\ n \in \mathbb{Z} \)
name: General Solution tanθ=tanα
subject: maths

text: Natural rubber is a polymer of:
explanation: Natural rubber: cis-1,4-polyisoprene (from isoprene, CH₂=C(CH₃)–CH=CH₂). Gutta percha is trans-1,4-polyisoprene (hard).
name: Natural Rubber Monomer
subject: che

text: Conservation of angular momentum: when a spinning ice skater pulls in arms, angular velocity:
options: Increases (I decreases, so ω increases to keep L constant)
explanation: \( L = I\omega = \text{const} \). Pulling arms in reduces \(I\), so \(\omega\) must increase. Classic example of angular momentum conservation.
name: Ice Skater Angular Momentum
subject: phy

text: The maximum value of \( -x^2 + 4x - 3 \) is:
explanation: Vertex at \( x = -\frac{b}{2a} = 2 \). Max value \( = -(4) + 8 - 3 = 1 \)
name: Maximum of Quadratic
subject: maths

text: The equation \( x^3 - 1 = 0 \) has how many real roots?
name: Roots of x³=1
subject: maths

text: Law of conservation of linear momentum holds when:
options: Net external force on the system is zero
All particles move with same velocity
explanation: Linear momentum is conserved when net external force = 0: \( \frac{d\vec{p}}{dt} = \vec{F}_{ext} = 0 \Rightarrow \vec{p} = \text{const} \).
name: Conservation of Momentum
subject: phy

text: Sandmeyer reaction converts diazonium salt to aryl halide using:
explanation: Sandmeyer: ArN₂⁺ + CuCl → ArCl + N₂. ArN₂⁺ + CuBr → ArBr + N₂. ArN₂⁺ + CuCN → ArCN + N₂. Replaces N₂ with X or CN.
name: Sandmeyer Reaction
subject: che

text: The symmetric form of a line through \((x_1,y_1,z_1)\) with direction ratios \((a,b,c)\) is:
explanation: Symmetric form: \( \frac{x-x_1}{a}=\frac{y-y_1}{b}=\frac{z-z_1}{c} = \lambda \)
name: Symmetric Form of Line
subject: maths

text: Bernoulli's equation for steady flow is:
explanation: Bernoulli's equation: \( P + \frac{1}{2}\rho v^2 + \rho g h = \text{const} \). Energy conservation for ideal fluid flow.
name: Bernoulli's Equation
subject: phy

text: Principal solution of \( \sin\theta = \frac{1}{2} \) in \( [0, 2\pi] \) are:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \frac{1}{2} \) in \([0,2\pi]\): \( \theta = \frac{\pi}{6} \) (1st quad) and \( \pi - \frac{\pi}{6} = \frac{5\pi}{6} \) (2nd quad).
name: Principal Solutions
subject: maths

text: Avogadro's law states that equal volumes of all gases at same T and P contain:
explanation: Avogadro's Law: equal volumes at same T, P → equal number of molecules (and moles). Both B and C are correct.
name: Avogadro's Law
subject: che

text: The slope of the \(x\)-axis is:
explanation: The \(x\)-axis is horizontal: slope \( = 0 \). The \(y\)-axis is vertical: slope undefined (\(\infty\)).
name: Slope of Axes
subject: maths

text: The Gibbs free energy change is given by:
explanation: \( \Delta G = \Delta H - T\Delta S \). Spontaneous process: \(\Delta G < 0\).
name: Gibbs Free Energy
subject: che

text: The standard equation of a hyperbola with transverse axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Hyperbola (transverse axis along x): \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \). Eccentricity \(e > 1\).
name: Hyperbola Standard Form
subject: maths

text: If \( \vec{a} = 2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+\hat{k} \) and \( \vec{b} = \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2\hat{k} \), then \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = \)
name: Dot Product Calculation
subject: maths

text: Faraday's first law of electromagnetic induction states:
options: Whenever magnetic flux through a circuit changes, an EMF is induced
Magnetic field is proportional to current
explanation: Faraday's 1st law: changing magnetic flux induces EMF. 2nd law: induced EMF \( = -\frac{d\Phi_B}{dt} \) (Faraday-Neumann law).
name: Faraday's First Law
subject: phy

text: The number of real roots of \( x^2 + |x| + 1 = 0 \) is:
explanation: For all real \(x\): \( x^2 \geq 0,\ |x| \geq 0 \), so \( x^2+|x|+1 \geq 1 > 0 \). No real roots.
name: No Real Roots
subject: maths

text: If the side of a square increases at 2 cm/s, the rate of increase of area when side = 5 cm is:
explanation: \( A=x^2 \Rightarrow \frac{dA}{dt} = 2x\frac{dx}{dt} = 2(5)(2) = 20 \text{ cm}^2/\text{s} \)
name: Related Rates
subject: maths

text: For a system \( AX = B \), if \( |A| \neq 0 \), the unique solution is:
explanation: Multiplying \( AX = B \) by \( A^{-1} \) on left: \( X = A^{-1}B \)
name: Cramer's Rule / Matrix Method
subject: maths

text: If \( A = \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 4 \end{pmatrix} \), then \( |A| = \)
name: 2x2 Determinant
subject: maths

text: Two coins are tossed. \( P(\text{exactly one head}) = \)
explanation: Sample space: \{HH, HT, TH, TT\}. Exactly one head: \{HT, TH\}. \( P = \frac{2}{4} = \frac{1}{2} \)
name: Coin Toss Probability
subject: maths

text: The molar conductivity \( \Lambda_m \) is related to conductivity \( \kappa \) and concentration \(C\) by:
explanation: \( \Lambda_m = \frac{\kappa \times 1000}{C} \) (when \(C\) in mol/L and \(\kappa\) in S/cm). Units: S·cm²/mol.
name: Molar Conductivity Formula
subject: che

text: Dispersion of white light in a prism is due to:
options: Different refractive indices for different wavelengths (n increases from red to violet)
explanation: Dispersion: different wavelengths have different \(n\). \(n_{violet} > n_{red}\). Violet bends most, red least. Causes rainbow, chromatic aberration.
name: Dispersion by Prism
subject: phy

text: The equation of tangent to \( y=f(x) \) at \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
name: Equation of Tangent
subject: maths

text: Conservative forces have the property that work done:
options: Is independent of path (depends only on initial and final positions)
Is always maximum on shortest path
explanation: Conservative force (gravity, spring, electrostatic): work done is path-independent. Non-conservative (friction): path-dependent.
name: Conservative Forces
subject: phy

text: The radius of circular motion of a charged particle in a magnetic field is:
explanation: Circular motion in \(B\): centripetal force = magnetic force: \( \frac{mv^2}{r} = qvB \Rightarrow r = \frac{mv}{qB} \). Used in mass spectrometer.
name: Radius of Circular Motion in B
subject: phy

text: The speed of sound in a gas (Newton-Laplace formula) is:
explanation: Laplace correction: \( v = \sqrt{\gamma P/\rho} \). Newton's formula lacked \(\gamma\) (assumed isothermal; actually adiabatic). \( v_{sound} \approx 332\,\text{m/s} \) at 0°C.
name: Speed of Sound in Gas
subject: phy

text: Avogadro's law (equal volumes at same T, P contain equal molecules) follows from kinetic theory because:
options: \( P = \frac{N}{3V}m\overline{v^2} = \frac{2N}{3V}\bar{E}_k \). If P, T same → N/V same
All gases have same molar mass
explanation: From kinetic theory: \( P = \frac{2n}{3}\bar{E}_k \). At same T, \( \bar{E}_k \) same. At same P, \( n = N/V \) same → equal molecules per volume.
name: Avogadro's Law from Kinetic Theory
subject: phy

text: The molar mass of \( \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \) is:
name: Molar Mass of H₂SO₄
subject: che

text: Geometric isomerism (cis-trans) is possible in square planar complexes of type:
explanation: cis-trans isomers in square planar: MA₂B₂ type. E.g., [Pt(NH₃)₂Cl₂]: cis (cisplatin, anticancer) and trans isomers.
name: Geometric Isomerism
subject: che

text: Two circles are orthogonal if:
explanation: Orthogonality condition: \( 2g_1g_2+2f_1f_2 = c_1+c_2 \)
name: Orthogonal Circles
subject: maths

text: The Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of a ketone with a peracid gives:
options: Ester (inserts oxygen into C–C bond next to carbonyl)
explanation: Baeyer-Villiger: R–CO–R' + peracid (mCPBA) → R–COO–R' (ester). An oxygen atom is inserted between carbonyl C and one alkyl group.
name: Baeyer-Villiger Oxidation
subject: che

text: The general term (\( T_{r+1} \)) in the expansion of \( (x+y)^n \) is:
explanation: By Binomial Theorem: \( T_{r+1} = \binom{n}{r} x^{n-r} y^r \)
name: General Term Formula
subject: maths

text: The SI unit of magnetic flux density (magnetic field) is:
explanation: Tesla (T) = Wb/m² = kg/(A·s²). Also: 1 T = 10⁴ Gauss. Earth's field ≈ 50 μT.
name: Unit of Magnetic Field
subject: phy

text: The potential due to a dipole at a point on its equatorial line (at distance \(r\)) is:
explanation: On equatorial line: potential \( V = 0 \). (Equal and opposite contributions from +q and -q). Field is non-zero but potential is zero.
name: Dipole Potential on Equatorial Line
subject: phy

text: The range of \( f(x) = x^2 - 4x + 5 \) is:
explanation: Minimum at \( x=2 \): \( f(2) = 4-8+5 = 1 \). Since \(a>0\), range is \([1, \infty)\)
name: Range of Quadratic
subject: maths

text: The modulus of \( z = \frac{1+2i}{3-4i} \) is:
name: Modulus of Complex Fraction
subject: maths

text: The sum \( 1^3 + 2^3 + \cdots + n^3 = \)
explanation: \( \sum_{k=1}^n k^3 = \left[\frac{n(n+1)}{2}\right]^2 \). The square of the sum of first \(n\) naturals.
name: Sum of Cubes
subject: maths

text: The transformer equation \( V_2/V_1 = N_2/N_1 \) comes from:
options: Faraday's law applied to both primary and secondary
explanation: Ideal transformer: same flux links both coils. \( \varepsilon_1 = N_1 d\Phi/dt \), \( \varepsilon_2 = N_2 d\Phi/dt \). Dividing: \( V_2/V_1 = N_2/N_1 \).
name: Transformer Equation Derivation
subject: phy

text: If rows of a determinant are interchanged, its value:
explanation: Interchanging any two rows (or columns) of a determinant changes its sign.
name: Row Interchange Property
subject: maths

text: The Cauchy-Schwarz inequality states: \( (a_1^2+a_2^2)(b_1^2+b_2^2) \geq \)
name: Cauchy-Schwarz
subject: maths

text: If \( P(A) = 0.4 \) and \( P(B) = 0.5 \) and \( A, B \) are mutually exclusive, then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
explanation: Mutually exclusive: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A)+P(B) = 0.4+0.5 = 0.9 \)
name: Mutually Exclusive Events
subject: maths

text: The Chain Rule: if \( y = f(g(x)) \), then \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \)
explanation: Chain Rule: \( \frac{dy}{dx} = f'(g(x))\cdot g'(x) \)
name: Chain Rule
subject: maths

text: Heat transfer by convection occurs primarily in:
explanation: Convection: heat transfer by movement of fluid itself. Natural (density difference) or forced. Cannot occur in solids or vacuum.
name: Convection in Fluids
subject: phy

text: The sum of focal distances from any point on ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: For any point \(P\) on ellipse: \( PF_1+PF_2 = 2a \) (constant). This defines the ellipse.
name: Focal Distance Sum
subject: maths

text: The resistivity \(\rho\) of a conductor is related to resistance \(R\) by:
explanation: Resistance: \( R = \frac{\rho L}{A} \), where \(L\) = length, \(A\) = cross-sectional area, \(\rho\) = resistivity.
name: Resistivity and Resistance
subject: phy

text: Photochemical smog involves:
options: NOₓ + unburnt hydrocarbons + UV → O₃, PAN, aldehydes
explanation: Photochemical smog: NOₓ + HC (UV light) → O₃ + PAN (peroxyacetyl nitrate) + formaldehyde. Brown haze, causes eye/lung irritation.
name: Photochemical Smog
subject: che

text: The relation between \( \Delta H \) and \( \Delta U \) is:
explanation: \( \Delta H = \Delta U + \Delta n_g RT \), where \(\Delta n_g\) = change in moles of gaseous species.
name: ΔH and ΔU Relation
subject: che

text: The locus of points equidistant from \( (2,0) \) and \( (-2,0) \) is:
explanation: Perpendicular bisector of segment from \((-2,0)\) to \((2,0)\) is the \(y\)-axis: \(x=0\).
name: Locus Equidistant
subject: maths

text: Electrophilic substitution (EAS) on benzene: the rate-determining step involves:
options: Loss of H⁺ from sigma complex
Addition of electrophile to all carbons
explanation: EAS mechanism: (1) Formation of π complex, (2) Formation of σ complex (arenium ion, slow, RDS), (3) Loss of H⁺ (fast) to restore aromaticity.
name: EAS Rate-Determining Step
subject: che

text: The lensmaker's equation is:
explanation: Lensmaker's equation: \( \frac{1}{f} = (n-1)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right) \). \(R_1\), \(R_2\) = radii of curvature of two surfaces.
name: Lensmaker's Equation
subject: phy

text: If \( f(x) \) is an even function, then \( \int_{-a}^{a} f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: Even function property: \( \int_{-a}^{a}f(x)dx = 2\int_0^a f(x)dx \)
name: Even Function Integral
subject: maths

text: The empirical formula represents:
options: The actual number of atoms in a molecule
The simplest whole number ratio of atoms
explanation: Empirical formula gives the simplest whole number ratio of atoms. Molecular formula = n × (empirical formula).
name: Empirical Formula
subject: che

text: \( \lim_{x \to a} \frac{x^n - a^n}{x - a} = \)
explanation: Standard algebraic limit: \( \lim_{x\to a}\frac{x^n-a^n}{x-a} = na^{n-1} \)
name: Algebraic Limit
subject: maths

text: The acidity order of alcohols and phenol is:
explanation: Acidity: PhOH(pKa ~10) > 1°ROH(~16) > 2°ROH(~17) > 3°ROH(~18). Phenoxide ion stabilised by resonance with aromatic ring.
name: Acidity Order
subject: che

text: \( \frac{1}{x(x+1)} = \frac{A}{x} + \frac{B}{x+1} \). What are \(A\) and \(B\)?
explanation: At \(x=0\): \(1 = A(1) \Rightarrow A=1\). At \(x=-1\): \(1 = B(-1) \Rightarrow B=-1\).
name: Basic Partial Fraction
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(a^x) = a^x\ln a \). When \(a=e\): reduces to \(e^x\).
name: Derivative of aˣ
subject: maths

text: If \( z = r(\cos\theta + i\sin\theta) \), then \( z^n \) by De-Moivre's theorem equals:
explanation: De-Moivre's Theorem: \( z^n = r^n(\cos n\theta + i\sin n\theta) \)
name: De-Moivre's Theorem
subject: maths

text: Gamma radiation is emitted when:
options: An excited nucleus transitions to lower energy state (no change in A or Z)
explanation: Gamma emission: excited nucleus (after \(\alpha\) or \(\beta\) decay or neutron capture) releases excess energy as \(\gamma\) photon. No change in A or Z.
name: Gamma Decay
subject: phy

text: The condition for \( lx+my+n=0 \) to be tangent to ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: Tangent condition to ellipse: \( a^2l^2+b^2m^2=n^2 \)
name: Tangent Condition to Ellipse
subject: maths

text: According to IUPAC, the longest carbon chain is chosen as the parent chain. In 2-methylpentane, the parent chain has:
explanation: 2-methylpentane: parent chain = pentane (5 carbons), with methyl substituent at C-2. Full name: 2-methylpentane.
name: Parent Chain Selection
subject: che

text: Fehling's solution is reduced by:
explanation: Fehling's test: gives brick-red Cu₂O precipitate with aliphatic aldehydes. Aromatic aldehydes (benzaldehyde) do NOT reduce Fehling's. Ketones: negative.
name: Fehling's Test
subject: che

text: \( \cos 2\theta = \)
explanation: \( \cos 2\theta = 2\cos^2\theta - 1 = 1 - 2\sin^2\theta = \cos^2\theta - \sin^2\theta \)
name: Double Angle - cos
subject: maths

text: The magnification produced by a lens is:
explanation: Lens magnification: \( m = v/u \). Positive: upright (virtual) image. Negative: inverted (real) image. Mirror: \( m = -v/u \).
name: Lens Magnification
subject: phy

text: The rank of a \( 3 \times 3 \) identity matrix \( I_3 \) is:
explanation: The identity matrix is full rank. For \( I_3 \): rank \( = 3 \) since \( |I_3| = 1 \neq 0 \).
name: Rank of Identity Matrix
subject: maths

text: The latus rectum of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) has length:
explanation: Length of latus rectum of \( y^2=4ax \) is \( 4a \) (chord through focus perpendicular to axis).
name: Latus Rectum of Parabola
subject: maths

text: The ratio of consecutive terms \( \frac{T_{r+1}}{T_r} \) in \( (1+x)^n \) is:
explanation: \( \frac{T_{r+1}}{T_r} = \frac{\binom{n}{r}}{\binom{n}{r-1}} x = \frac{n-r+1}{r} x \). Used to find greatest term.
name: Ratio of Consecutive Terms
subject: maths

text: The equation of a plane in normal form is:
explanation: Normal form of plane: \( lx+my+nz=p \), where \(l,m,n\) are direction cosines of normal and \(p>0\).
name: Plane Normal Form
subject: maths

text: The DE whose solution is \( y = Ae^{2x}+Be^{-2x} \) is:
explanation: \( y'' = 4Ae^{2x}+4Be^{-2x} = 4y \Rightarrow y''-4y=0 \)
name: DE from Solution
subject: maths

explanation: Special formula: \( \int e^x[f(x)+f'(x)]dx = e^xf(x)+C \). Very useful shortcut.
name: eˣ[f+f'] Integration
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta, \gamma \) are roots of \( x^3 + px + q = 0 \), then \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 + \gamma^2 = \)
explanation: \( \alpha^2+\beta^2+\gamma^2 = (\alpha+\beta+\gamma)^2 - 2(\alpha\beta+\beta\gamma+\gamma\alpha) = 0 - 2p = -2p \)
name: Sum of Squares of Roots
subject: maths

text: If \( f(x) \) is an odd function, then \( \int_{-a}^{a} f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: Odd function property: \( \int_{-a}^{a}f(x)dx = 0 \) (symmetry about origin cancels)
name: Odd Function Integral
subject: maths

text: The number of orbitals in a subshell with \(l=2\) (d subshell) is:
explanation: Number of orbitals in a subshell \( = 2l+1 \). For \(l=2\): \(2(2)+1=5\) orbitals.
name: Orbitals in d Subshell
subject: che

text: Hydrogen bonding is strongest in:
explanation: H-bonding strength depends on electronegativity and size of atom. F is most electronegative and smallest → strongest H-bond in HF.
name: Strongest Hydrogen Bond
subject: che

text: In triangle \(ABC\), \( A + B + C = \)
explanation: Sum of angles in any triangle \( = 180° = \pi \) radians.
name: Angle Sum Property
subject: maths

text: The product formula \( 2\sin A \cos B = \)
explanation: Product-to-Sum: \( 2\sin A\cos B = \sin(A+B) + \sin(A-B) \)
name: Product-to-Sum
subject: maths

text: The relation between \(K_p\) and \(K_c\) is:
explanation: \( K_p = K_c(RT)^{\Delta n_g} \), where \(\Delta n_g\) = moles of gaseous products − moles of gaseous reactants.
name: Kp and Kc Relation
subject: che

text: If the length of a simple pendulum is quadrupled, its time period:
explanation: \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{l/g} \propto \sqrt{l} \). If \( l \to 4l \): \( T \to 2\pi\sqrt{4l/g} = 2T \). Period doubles.
name: Effect of Length on Pendulum Period
subject: phy

text: Quantisation of charge means electric charge exists in multiples of:
explanation: Charge quantisation: \( q = ne \), where \( n \in \mathbb{Z} \) and \( e = 1.6\times10^{-19}\,\text{C} \). Millikan's oil drop experiment verified this.
name: Charge Quantisation
subject: phy

text: The angle between \( \vec{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j} \) and \( \vec{b}=\hat{j}+\hat{k} \) is:
explanation: \( \vec{a}\cdot\vec{b} = 0+1+0=1 \). \( |\vec{a}|=\sqrt{2}, |\vec{b}|=\sqrt{2} \). \( \cos\theta = \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \theta=60° \)
name: Angle Between Vectors
subject: maths

text: The number of unknown constants in the partial fraction of \( \frac{f(x)}{(x-1)^2(x^2+4)} \) is:
explanation: \( \frac{A}{x-1} + \frac{B}{(x-1)^2} + \frac{Cx+D}{x^2+4} \) — 4 unknowns: \(A, B, C, D\).
name: Counting Constants
subject: maths

text: Area of circle \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) using integration is:
explanation: Area \( = 4\int_0^r \sqrt{r^2-x^2}\,dx = \pi r^2 \). Verified by standard integration.
name: Area of Circle
subject: maths

text: For the reaction \( 2\text{SO}_2 + \text{O}_2 \rightleftharpoons 2\text{SO}_3 \), if \(K_c = 4\) for the forward reaction, then \(K_c\) for the reverse reaction is:
explanation: For reverse reaction: \( K_c' = \frac{1}{K_c} = \frac{1}{4} = 0.25 \).
name: K for Reverse Reaction
subject: che

text: From a pack of 52 cards, probability of drawing a king is:
explanation: 4 kings in 52 cards: \( P = \frac{4}{52} = \frac{1}{13} \)
name: Card Probability
subject: maths

text: The standard equation of a parabola with axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Standard parabola (opens right): \( y^2 = 4ax \), focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \( x = -a \).
name: Parabola Standard Form
subject: maths

text: Nucleophilic aromatic substitution on aryl halides is facilitated by:
explanation: Nucleophilic aromatic substitution (SNAr): electron-withdrawing groups (–NO₂) ortho/para to leaving group stabilise the Meisenheimer complex intermediate.
name: Nucleophilic Aromatic Substitution
subject: che

text: Huygens' principle states that every point on a wavefront acts as:
options: A source of secondary spherical wavelets
explanation: Huygens' principle: each point on a primary wavefront is a source of secondary wavelets. The new wavefront is the envelope of these secondary wavelets.
name: Huygens' Principle
subject: phy

text: The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter is:
explanation: Solid sphere (diameter): \( I = \frac{2}{5}MR^2 \). Hollow sphere: \( \frac{2}{3}MR^2 \). Solid disc: \( \frac{1}{2}MR^2 \).
name: MOI of Solid Sphere
subject: phy

text: A catalyst increases the rate of reaction by:
options: Providing an alternative pathway with lower activation energy
explanation: Catalyst provides alternate reaction pathway with lower \(E_a\). More molecules have enough energy to react.
name: How Catalyst Works
subject: che

text: The normal form of a line is \( x\cos\alpha + y\sin\alpha = p \). Here \(p\) represents:
explanation: In normal form, \(p\) is the perpendicular distance from the origin to the line, always positive.
name: Normal Form
subject: maths

text: The polar form of \( z = 1 + i \) is:
name: Polar Form
subject: maths

text: The angle made by the tangent to \( y=x^2 \) at \( (1,1) \) with x-axis is:
name: Angle of Tangent
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( x^2 - px + q = 0 \), then \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = \)
explanation: \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = (\alpha+\beta)^2 - 2\alpha\beta = p^2 - 2q \)
name: Sum of Squares of Roots
subject: maths

text: The phase difference between voltage and current in a pure inductor is:
explanation: Pure inductor: \( V = L\frac{dI}{dt} \). If \( I = I_0\sin\omega t \), then \( V = LI_0\omega\cos\omega t \). Voltage leads current by 90°.
name: Phase in Pure Inductor
subject: phy

text: The total probability theorem states \( P(B) = \sum P(A_i) \cdot P(B|A_i) \) when \( A_i \) form:
explanation: The theorem applies when \( A_1, A_2, \ldots \) form a mutually exclusive and exhaustive partition of the sample space.
name: Total Probability Theorem
subject: maths

text: In E2 elimination, the leaving group and the β-hydrogen must be:
explanation: E2 (bimolecular elimination): concerted, requires anti-periplanar arrangement (180° dihedral) of H and leaving group for effective overlap.
name: E2 Stereochemistry
subject: che

text: The threshold frequency \(\nu_0\) in photoelectric effect is:
options: Minimum frequency for any photoelectric emission: \( \phi_0 = h\nu_0 \)
Frequency at which KE is maximum
explanation: Threshold frequency: minimum frequency below which no emission occurs regardless of intensity. \( \nu_0 = \phi_0/h \).
name: Threshold Frequency
subject: phy

text: The van't Hoff factor \(i\) for \( \text{NaCl} \) in dilute solution is approximately:
explanation: NaCl → Na⁺ + Cl⁻ (2 ions). \(i ≈ 2\) for complete dissociation. Actual value slightly less due to ion pairing.
name: Van't Hoff Factor
subject: che

text: Optical fibre communication works on the principle of:
options: Total internal reflection (light confined inside fibre core)
explanation: Optical fibre: light undergoes TIR inside core (high n) surrounded by cladding (lower n). Very high bandwidth, low loss, immune to EM interference.
name: Optical Fibre Principle
subject: phy

text: The moment of inertia of a thin rod about an axis through its centre perpendicular to its length is:
explanation: Thin rod (centre, perpendicular): \( I = \frac{ML^2}{12} \). About one end: \( I = \frac{ML^2}{3} \).
name: MOI of Thin Rod
subject: phy

text: The range of \( \tan^{-1}x \) is:
explanation: \( \tan^{-1}x \) has range \( \left(-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right) \) (open interval, excluding endpoints).
name: Range of tan⁻¹
subject: maths

text: The acidity of carboxylic acids compared to alcohols is due to:
explanation: RCOOH (pKa ~5) >> ROH (pKa ~16). The carboxylate anion –COO⁻ is resonance-stabilised (negative charge delocalised over 2 oxygens).
name: Acidity of Carboxylic Acids
subject: che

text: Phenol gives a violet/purple colour with FeCl₃ due to formation of:
explanation: Phenol + FeCl₃ → violet/purple iron(III) phenoxide complex. Used as a test for phenols. Enols also give positive test.
name: FeCl₃ Test for Phenol
subject: che

text: The correct order of electromagnetic spectrum from lowest to highest frequency is:
options: Gamma > X-ray > UV > Visible > IR > Microwave > Radio
Radio < Microwave < IR < Visible < UV < X-ray < Gamma
UV < Visible < IR < Microwave < Radio < X-ray < Gamma
X-ray > Visible > Radio > Gamma > IR > Microwave
explanation: EM spectrum (increasing frequency): Radio < Microwave < Infrared < Visible < UV < X-ray < Gamma rays.
name: EM Spectrum Order
subject: phy

text: The electronic configuration of \( \text{Fe}^{2+} \) (Fe: atomic number 26) is:
explanation: Fe: \([\text{Ar}]\,3d^6 4s^2\). \(\text{Fe}^{2+}\) loses 2 electrons from 4s first: \([\text{Ar}]\,3d^6\).
name: Electronic Configuration of Ion
subject: che

text: \( \sin 2\theta = \)
explanation: \( \sin 2\theta = 2\sin\theta\cos\theta \). Also \( = \frac{2\tan\theta}{1+\tan^2\theta} \).
name: Double Angle - sin
subject: maths

text: The relation between Y, B (bulk modulus), and G (shear modulus) through Poisson's ratio \(\nu\) is:
explanation:
Elastic moduli relations: \( Y = 2G(1+
u) \) and \( Y = 3B(1-2
u) \). For incompressible material (\(
u=0.5\)): \(B	o\infty\).
name: Relation Between Elastic Moduli
subject: phy

text: The number of ways of selecting at least one from 3 items is:
explanation: Total subsets \( = 2^3 = 8 \). Excluding empty set: \( 2^3 - 1 = 7 \)
name: Select At Least One
subject: maths

text: The number of significant figures in 0.00450 is:
explanation: Leading zeros are not significant. In 0.00450: 4, 5, 0 (trailing zero after decimal) = 3 significant figures.
name: Significant Figures
subject: phy

text: Emulsions are colloidal systems of:
explanation: Emulsion: liquid dispersed in another liquid. Types: O/W (oil in water, e.g., milk) and W/O (water in oil, e.g., butter).
name: Emulsion Definition
subject: che

text: The coordinates of a point in the third quadrant satisfy:
explanation: Third quadrant: both \(x\) and \(y\) are negative (\(x<0, y<0\)).
name: Quadrant Sign Convention
subject: maths

text: \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{e^x - 1}{x} = \)
explanation: Standard exponential limit: \( \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{e^x-1}{x} = 1 \)
name: Exponential Limit
subject: maths

text: Ozone (O₃) is a better oxidising agent than O₂ because:
explanation: O₃ → O₂ + [O]. Nascent oxygen [O] is highly reactive oxidising agent. This explains O₃'s disinfecting and bleaching ability.
name: Ozone as Oxidising Agent
subject: che

text: Variable oxidation states in transition elements are due to:
options: Small energy difference between 3d and 4s orbitals allowing multiple electron losses
explanation: 3d and 4s orbitals have similar energies, so various numbers of electrons can be lost. E.g., Mn shows +2 to +7 oxidation states.
name: Variable Oxidation States
subject: che

text: The combined equation \( ax^2 + 2hxy + by^2 = 0 \) represents a pair of lines through origin. The angle \(\theta\) between them satisfies:
explanation: Angle between pair of lines: \( \tan\theta = \frac{2\sqrt{h^2-ab}}{a+b} \)
name: Angle Between Pair of Lines
subject: maths

text: Beats are produced when two sound waves of frequencies \(f_1\) and \(f_2\) are superposed. Beat frequency equals:
explanation: Beat frequency \( f_{beat} = |f_1 - f_2| \). Perceptible as beats when difference is small (< ~7 Hz for human ear).
name: Beat Frequency
subject: phy

text: The number of ways to distribute 5 distinct balls into 3 distinct boxes is:
explanation: Each of 5 balls has 3 choices. Total \( = 3^5 = 243 \)
name: Distribution in Boxes
subject: maths

text: The area of triangle with \( a=5, b=6, c=7 \) using Heron's formula (\(s=9\)) is:
explanation: \( s=9 \). \( \Delta = \sqrt{9 \cdot 4 \cdot 3 \cdot 2} = \sqrt{216} = 6\sqrt{6} \). Both A and B are correct.
name: Heron's Calculation
subject: maths

text: Weightlessness in a satellite is because:
options: Both satellite and person are in free fall (same centripetal acceleration)
explanation: In orbit: both person and satellite have same centripetal acceleration. Person is in continuous free fall → apparent weightlessness.
name: Weightlessness in Orbit
subject: phy

text: Interhalogen compounds like ClF₃ are formed because:
options: Larger halogens can expand their octet using d orbitals
explanation: Interhalogens: formed between two different halogens. Larger halogens (Cl, Br, I) use d orbitals to accommodate more F atoms. E.g., IF₇.
name: Interhalogen Compounds
subject: che

text: The inverse of \( f(x) = 2x + 3 \) is:
explanation: Set \( y = 2x+3 \Rightarrow x = \frac{y-3}{2} \). So \( f^{-1}(x) = \frac{x-3}{2} \)
name: Inverse Function
subject: maths

text: Acetylation of aniline (PhNH₂) with acetic anhydride gives:
explanation: PhNH₂ + (CH₃CO)₂O → PhNH–COCH₃ (acetanilide) + CH₃COOH. Protects amino group from oxidation during synthesis.
name: Acetylation of Aniline
subject: che

text: Soft iron is preferred for making electromagnets because it has:
options: Low coercivity and low retentivity (easily magnetised/demagnetised)
explanation: Soft iron: low coercivity (easily demagnetised), low retentivity → ideal for electromagnets (must lose magnetism when current stops). Steel (hard): high coercivity → permanent magnets.
name: Soft Iron for Electromagnets
subject: phy

text: The general solution of \( \sin\theta = \sin\alpha \) is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \sin\alpha \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + (-1)^n\alpha,\ n \in \mathbb{Z} \)
name: General Solution sinθ=sinα
subject: maths

text: Atomic radius across a period (left to right) generally:
explanation: Atomic radius decreases across a period due to increasing nuclear charge (more protons attract electrons more strongly).
name: Atomic Radius Trend
subject: che

text: The unit vector in direction of \( \vec{a} = 3\hat{i}-4\hat{k} \) is:
explanation: \( |\vec{a}| = \sqrt{9+16}=5 \). Unit vector \( \hat{a} = \frac{\vec{a}}{|\vec{a}|} = \frac{3\hat{i}-4\hat{k}}{5} \)
name: Unit Vector
subject: maths

text: The work done in moving a charge \(q\) through potential difference \(V\) is:
explanation: \( W = q\Delta V = qV \). 1 eV = energy gained by electron through 1V = \( 1.6\times10^{-19}\,\text{J} \).
name: Work in Moving Charge
subject: phy

text: For \( f(x) = x\sin\frac{1}{x} \), \( \lim_{x\to 0}f(x) = \)
explanation: \( |x\sin\frac{1}{x}| \leq |x| \to 0 \). By squeeze theorem, \( \lim_{x\to 0}x\sin\frac{1}{x} = 0 \).
name: Oscillating Limit
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{x}{y} \) is:
explanation: Separate: \( y\,dy = x\,dx \Rightarrow \frac{y^2}{2}=\frac{x^2}{2}+C_1 \Rightarrow y^2=x^2+C \)
name: Separable Variables
subject: maths

text: The number of handshakes when 10 people each shake hands with every other is:
explanation: Each handshake involves 2 people: \( ^{10}C_2 = 45 \)
name: Handshakes Problem
subject: maths

text: The term independent of \(x\) in \( \left(x + \frac{1}{x}\right)^6 \) is:
name: Term Independent of x
subject: maths

text: Electronegativity increases in the order:
explanation: Electronegativity: F(4.0) > O(3.5) > N(3.0) > Cl(3.0). F is the most electronegative element.
name: Electronegativity Order
subject: che

text: The value of \( i^{97} \) is:
explanation: \( i^{97} = i^{4 \times 24 + 1} = i^1 = i \). Powers of \(i\) cycle with period 4.
name: Powers of i
subject: maths
